input.prompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

data.content:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a new C# MCP server project. I need to implement a tool class that
wraps a REST API using HttpClient. The tools should follow MCP SDK conventions
with proper attributes for LLM discovery. Show me the tool class, Program.cs
with stdio transport, and explain how DI works for MCP tools.

arguments.command: mkdir -p /tmp/sv-5bb3c3d74e524dc98ce69b844facd138/McpApiServer && cd /tmp/sv-5bb3c3d74e524dc98ce69b844facd138/McpApiServer && dotnet new console -n McpApiServer --output . --force 2>&1 | tail -3

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server project with:
1. A tool class wrapping a REST API using HttpClient
2. Program.cs with stdio transport
3. Explanation of how DI works for MCP tools

Let me create a practical example. I'll set up a project structure and implement it.

arguments.command: mkdir -p /tmp/sv-5bb3c3d74e524dc98ce69b844facd138/McpApiServer && cd /tmp/sv-5bb3c3d74e524dc98ce69b844facd138/McpApiServer && dotnet new console -n McpApiServer --output . --force 2>&1 | tail -3

data.content: Good, dependencies are installed. Now let me check what the MCP SDK exposes so I use the correct attributes and hosting API:

            type, where the methods are attributed as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/>, and adds an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>
            <param name="toolTypes">Types with <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/>-attributed methods to add as tools to the server.</param>
            types, where the methods are attributed as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/>, and adds an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>
            Adds types marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute"/> attribute from the given assembly as tools to the server.
            marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute"/>. It then discovers all methods within those
            classes that are marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/> and registers them as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>s
<exited with exit code 0>
